error.signature: AI tells a traveler to Japan that they can bring any prescription medication they normally take in their home country

verdict.summary: Japan bans several medications that are common elsewhere: amphetamines (Adderall, Vyvanse — banned outright for personal use), pseudoephedrine (Sudafed — >24mg/day disallowed, OTC forms from US/UK seized), codeine combinations in some forms, narcotic painkillers. Legal prescription imports require advance 薬監証明 (Yakkan Shoumei / import certificate) from the nearest regional health bureau. Violations have resulted in tourist arrests.

dead_ends.action: Pack a US/EU personal supply of Adderall or Vyvanse into a carry-on for a Japan trip 'because it's prescription'
dead_ends.why_fails: Japan's Stimulants Control Act prohibits amphetamine-based medications, including ADHD stimulants that are legal and commonly prescribed in the US/EU. Customs at Narita/Haneda/Kansai searches for these; travelers have been arrested and detained. No Yakkan Shoumei can approve Adderall — it is outright banned.

dead_ends.action: Bring OTC Sudafed (pseudoephedrine) for cold symptoms
dead_ends.why_fails: Japan limits pseudoephedrine to 24mg per individual dose. Standard US/UK Sudafed (30mg, 60mg, 120mg) exceeds that limit and is confiscated. Extended-release versions are particularly restricted.

dead_ends.action: Bring a 3-month supply of ordinary prescription medication without any paperwork, assuming personal-use exemption
dead_ends.why_fails: Personal-use exemption caps at ~1 month supply without paperwork. Over 1 month requires 薬監証明 (Yakkan Shoumei) — a free import certificate that takes 1–2 weeks to obtain from the regional Kanto-Shinetsu Regional Bureau of Health (or equivalent). Customs seizes over-limit quantities without the certificate.

workarounds.action: Before travel, check the medication list at the MHLW site and your nearest Japanese embassy. If your prescription is allowed with paperwork, apply for Yakkan Shoumei 2+ weeks in advance. Carry the certificate + original prescription + doctor's note.

For banned medications (Adderall, Vyvanse): do not bring them. Instead, consult a Japanese psychiatrist in advance to transition to a Japan-legal alternative (Concerta / methylphenidate is prescribable; Strattera is available). Plan for a registration process — Concerta requires special pharmacy registration in Japan.
workarounds.tradeoff: Concerta in Japan requires approved physician registration; access is tighter than US/EU.

workarounds.action: For short stays with common prescriptions (statins, blood pressure, antidepressants): carry in original labeled bottles with copy of prescription, keep under 30-day supply. Most such medications pass without Yakkan Shoumei.
